outtro.p: Note that the summands of \renewcommand{\log}{\ln}\displaystyle \left({{T0}}\right) + \left({{T1}}\right) +\left({{T2}}\right) +\left({{T3}}\right)+\left({{T4}}\right) +\cdots follows the pattern of \renewcommand{\log}{\ln}\displaystyle {{Ax}} where x={{Input}} and n starts at {{k0}}.

Since \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle {{fx}} = \sum_{ n = {{k1}} }^\infty {{ax}}, we have that \renewcommand{\log}{\ln}\displaystyle F(x) = {{Fx}} = \sum_{ n = {{k0}} }^\infty {{Ax}}. So \renewcommand{\log}{\ln}\displaystyle \left({{T0}}\right) + \left({{T1}}\right) +\left({{T2}}\right) +\left({{T3}}\right)+\left({{T4}}\right) +\cdots = F\left({{Input}}\right) = {{Limit}}.
